goal: Make /app/followups read like a relationship follow-through workspace for deciding what promise to keep next, while preserving all local, side-effect-free behavior.

success_criteria.description: GET /app/followups first viewport leads with one current follow-through priority: who the promise is for, why it matters now, the source trigger, draft readiness, reminder timing, and the next safe action before broader task or queue inventory.

Visible follow-up task, message draft, reminder, and queue items are grouped into a readable promise-to-send workflow with relationship labels, due timing, source context, and clear review status rather than technical inventory.

GET /app/followups?action=complete-top-followup shows a usable completion preview that confirms the selected promise, draft or reminder context, and exactly what remains staged locally; reloading the same action route remains stable.

The page and action preview explicitly show that no saved record, calendar or scheduler change, email/message send, notification delivery, automated writing call, or outside network request occurs; raw queue/evidence IDs are not dominant primary copy.

GET /app/followups?scenario=empty, pending, failure keep meaningful recovery controls; page tests assert the promise workflow, action safety ledger, route recovery, mobile wrapping guardrails, and copy guardrails; npm test and npm run build pass with no feature, service, API, fixture, shared UI, shell, contact, event, chat, dashboard, agent, profile, or home route edits.

out_of_scope: Changing feature contracts, services, service factories, API envelopes, route handlers, or sample data fixtures
Changing global app shell, global CSS, shared UI primitives, contacts, events, profile, chat, dashboard, agent, or home routes
Adding live email, calendar, CRM, notification, messaging, scheduler, storage, search, AI, or network integrations
Changing task, notification, or draft generation feature behavior outside the route composition layer
